What is related here and in the rest of the chapters until the end of this book happened soon after the death of Joshua; see chapter . To show how happy the nation was under the Judges, it is shown here how unhappy they were when there was no Judge. The love of money made Micah so disobedient to his mother that he robbed her, and made her so unkind to her son that she cursed him. Losses drive good people to prayer, but bad people to curses. This woman’s silver was her god before it became a graven or molten image. Micah and his mother agreed to turn their money into a god and set up idol worship in their family. See the cause of this corruption: Every man did what was right in his own eyes, and then they soon did what was evil in the sight of the Lord.

Micah thought it was a sign of God’s favor to him and his images that a Levite should come to his door. Those who please themselves with their own delusions are apt to think that God is pleased with them if Providence unexpectedly brings anything to their hands that further them in their evil way.
